title: Fix CSV/XLS import failure — all records failing to upsert to backend

requirements.summary: Fix import failure in CSVImportSection where all parsed records fail to upsert to the backend, add detailed per-record error logging, and ensure error toast messages include specific backend error reasons
acceptanceCriteria: Importing valid XLS files with unit IDs like S10026_PRD, S10006-PRD, S10002-PRD for week 2026-W09 successfully upserts records to the backend without error
The 'All N records failed to import' toast no longer appears for valid files
If a record does fail, the error toast includes the specific backend error message (not just the unit ID and filename)
Successfully imported records appear on the Dashboard page under the correct week

file_operations.description: Investigate and fix the backend upsert failure by examining how the component calls the upsertReport mutation. Add detailed console logging before each upsert attempt (logging unitId, weekYear, model, totalPkts, storedPkts, validGpsFixPkts). Ensure the unit ID format matches what the backend expects (check for extra spaces, case sensitivity, or character encoding issues). Verify that the model enum value from the selector matches the UnitModel type exactly. Ensure weekYear is in the correct ISO week format (YYYY-Www). Capture and log the specific error thrown by the backend actor during upsert, then include that error message in the toast notification so users see the root cause (not just 'All N records failed to import'). Test with the provided sample files (S10026_PRD.xls, S10006-PRD.xls, S10002-PRD.xls) to confirm successful import and verify records appear on the Dashboard under week 2026-W09.
